name: San Michele Cemetery Extension
writeup: Chipperfield designed an extension to the cemetery island of San Michele, the historic burial place of Venice, adding new burial courts and facilities to the western end of the island. The new courts use plain white concrete walls to create enclosed spaces of great serenity that are consistent with the scale and atmosphere of the existing cemetery without imitating its historical language. The geometry of the new courts was carefully calibrated against the footprint of the existing buildings across the water.
detail: The new burial courts are entirely enclosed by plain concrete walls that frame views of the sky above, creating spaces of defined geometry and complete serenity that belong visually to the island's white walls.

images.caption: Isola di San Michele from above, Venice
